that made you.

Before you scroll — one breath

ready?
The method

Five inputs. One whole you.

Your body runs on what nature gives it every day. Eco-skills turns each input into a small, precise practice.

Breath

The 1 : 4 : 2 rhythm that resets your nervous system.

Eco-respiration

Slow nasal breathing in a 1 : 4 : 2 ratio — inhale, hold, release — oxygenates the blood, raises cellular energy and quietens the stress response.

Practice: ten slow rounds, just after waking.

The founder

The scientist who listened to a river.

Nature has a solution to every problem.

For over five decades at Banaras Hindu University, Prof. Tripathi studied how the Ganga heals itself — research recognised by the Parliament of India and foundational to the national Ganga Action Plan. Eco-skills is that life's work distilled: the laws by which nature restores a river, turned into a daily practice that restores a person.

0years of research
0research papers

0people studied
Your first week

Seven days to begin.

The free trial walks the same path as the full programme — body, breath, the elements, the mind, the self. Choose a day to see what it holds.

Day 1Awaken the Body

Touch the earth on waking, rub the palms and clap ten times, then gentle head, neck and joint vibrations — switching the whole body on.

What changes

Benefits that build.

Reported by participants and observed across a six-month study of four hundred people.

Physical health

More energy, better circulation and immunity, relief from aches, steadier blood pressure.

A quiet mind

Lower stress and anxiety, gentler sleep, a steadier response to difficult days.

Focus & memory

Eco-centralization trains deep observation — sharper concentration and recall.

Daily energy

Sun, water and breath used at the right time of day — energy that doesn't crash.

Self-confidence

Knowing your interests, skills and values — and naming your ultimate goal.

A whole personality

The five dimensions converge — physical, mental, self-aware, focused, growing.

Guidance & care

Our expert counsellors.

A dedicated team with decades of scholarship in sociology, social medicine and human development — and a shared commitment to your wellbeing.

Prof. B. D. Tripathi

Founder & Chief Mentor

UGC Emeritus Professor and Chairman of the Mahamana Malaviya Ganga Research Centre, BHU. A pioneer of Ganga river research recognised by the Parliament of India — 226+ papers, 10 books, 32 PhDs guided. Eco-skills is the distilled essence of his life's work.

Dr. Suman Tiwari

Director — Admin

Expert in Environmental Sociology — M.A. (1993) and Ph.D. (1997), Department of Sociology, BHU. Former Women Coordinator at the National Environmental Conservation Association and Programme Coordinator in the Netherlands-aided Jyoti Mahila Nirbal Programme.

Dr. Sarita Pandey

Director — Planning

Ph.D. in Preventive & Social Medicine, Institute of Medical Sciences, BHU. Two decades of work in girl-child education, nutrition and rural self-help groups; academic study across Italy, France, Austria, Germany, Armenia and the USA on a UN J-visa.

Ms. Jyoti Tripathi

Director — HR

Science graduate with an MBA in Human Resource Management (IMS, Varanasi). Four years at Sai College for Women, Bangalore; leads workshops on organisational behaviour, with deep experience in women's empowerment and children's learning.

More experienced counsellors are joining our panel very soon.
